name: SNAP (Food Stamps)
description: Federal nutrition assistance program providing monthly benefits for groceries
incomeLimitNotes: Gross monthly income at or below 130% of the Federal Poverty Level

name: WIC (Women, Infants, and Children)
description: Nutrition assistance for pregnant/postpartum women, infants, and children under 5
eligibilitySummary: Pregnant/postpartum women, children under 5, income ≤ 185% FPL
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 185% of the Federal Poverty Level

name: National School Lunch Program
description: Free and reduced-price school meals for eligible students
incomeLimitNotes: Free meals: income ≤ 130% FPL; Reduced-price: income ≤ 185% FPL

name: Feeding America
description: National food bank network with 200+ food banks across the country
eligibilitySummary: No income requirement — anyone in need

name: TEFAP (The Emergency Food Assistance Program)
description: USDA commodity food distribution through local agencies
incomeLimitNotes: Eligibility criteria set by individual states

name: Meals on Wheels
description: Home-delivered meals for seniors who are homebound

name: CSFP (Commodity Supplemental Food Program)
description: Monthly food packages for low-income seniors
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 130% of the Federal Poverty Level

name: HUD Rental Assistance
description: Section 8 housing choice vouchers and public housing programs
incomeLimitNotes: Generally income at or below 50% of the Area Median Income

name: HUD Housing Counseling
description: Free housing counselors nationwide for renters and homebuyers

name: National Low Income Housing Coalition
description: Housing resource database and advocacy for affordable housing

name: USDA Rural Housing
description: Loans and grants for housing in rural areas
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary by county and household size

name: HUD Emergency Solutions Grants
description: Homelessness prevention and rapid re-housing assistance
eligibilitySummary: At risk of homelessness or currently homeless

name: National Alliance to End Homelessness
description: Resource finder for people experiencing homelessness
eligibilitySummary: Anyone experiencing or at risk of homelessness

name: Habitat for Humanity
description: Affordable homeownership program through volunteer-built homes
eligibilitySummary: Income 30-60% AMI, varies by local affiliate
incomeLimitNotes: Income between 30-60% of Area Median Income

name: CareerOneStop
description: DOL job search, career exploration, and training resources

name: American Job Centers (AJCs)
description: In-person employment services, resume help, and job training

name: Unemployment Insurance
description: Temporary income for workers who lost their job through no fault of their own
eligibilitySummary: Recently employed, lost job through no fault of own, varies by state

name: WIOA (Workforce Innovation and Opportunity Act)
description: Federally funded job training and employment programs

name: Job Corps
description: Free education and vocational training for young people
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below the poverty level

name: TANF (Temporary Assistance for Needy Families)
description: Cash assistance and support services for families with children
eligibilitySummary: Families with children, income limits vary by state
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits set by individual states

name: SSI (Supplemental Security Income)
description: Monthly cash payments for disabled, blind, or elderly individuals with limited income
eligibilitySummary: Disabled, blind, or age 65+, with limited income and resources
incomeLimitNotes: Limited countable income and resources under $2,000 individual / $3,000 couple

name: SSDI (Social Security Disability Insurance)
description: Disability benefits for workers with qualifying work history
eligibilitySummary: Work history required, plus a qualifying disability

name: Earned Income Tax Credit (EITC)
description: Tax credit for low-to-moderate income working individuals and families
eligibilitySummary: Working individuals/families, income limits vary by filing status and dependents
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits vary; e.g., ~$63,398 for married filing jointly with 3+ children (2024)

name: Medicaid
description: Free or low-cost health coverage for eligible low-income individuals
eligibilitySummary: Income-based, varies by state (many states cover adults up to 138% FPL)
incomeLimitNotes: Expansion states: income ≤ 138% FPL for adults

name: CHIP (Children's Health Insurance Program)
description: Health insurance for children in families with incomes too high for Medicaid
eligibilitySummary: Children in families above Medicaid income limits but below CHIP limits
incomeLimitNotes: Typically up to 200-300% FPL depending on state

name: Healthcare.gov Marketplace
description: ACA health insurance marketplace with income-based subsidies
eligibilitySummary: Anyone can apply; subsidies are income-based
incomeLimitNotes: Premium tax credits available for income 100-400% FPL

name: HRSA Health Centers
description: Federally qualified community health centers with sliding-scale fees
eligibilitySummary: Anyone — sliding fee scale based on ability to pay

name: 988 Suicide & Crisis Lifeline
description: 24/7 free mental health crisis support via call, text, or chat
eligibilitySummary: Anyone in crisis — no eligibility requirement

name: SAMHSA Treatment Locator
description: Find substance abuse and mental health treatment facilities

name: NeedyMeds
description: Database of patient assistance programs for prescription medications

name: RxAssist
description: Comprehensive database of pharmaceutical patient assistance programs

name: Hill-Burton Free Care
description: Hospitals and facilities obligated to provide free or reduced-cost care
eligibilitySummary: Income-based at participating Hill-Burton obligated facilities
incomeLimitNotes: Typically income at or below the Federal Poverty Level

name: FTA Programs (Federal Transit Administration)
description: Federal transit assistance and grant programs overview

name: National RTAP (Rural Transit Assistance Program)
description: Transit assistance and training resources for rural communities
eligibilitySummary: Rural residents and rural transit providers

name: Good News Garage
description: Vehicle donation program providing cars to working families in need
eligibilitySummary: Working poor in select states (VT, NH, MA, CT, NJ, NY)

name: Working Cars for Working People
description: Vehicle assistance program for employment-verified individuals

name: LIHEAP (Low Income Home Energy Assistance Program)
description: Helps low-income households pay heating and cooling bills
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 150% of the Federal Poverty Level (some states higher)

name: Lifeline
description: Discounted monthly phone or internet service for qualifying households
eligibilitySummary: Income ≤ 135% FPL or participation in qualifying programs (SNAP, Medicaid, etc.)
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 135% of the Federal Poverty Level

name: ACP (Affordable Connectivity Program)
description: $30/month discount on broadband internet service
eligibilitySummary: Income ≤ 200% FPL or participation in qualifying programs
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 200% of the Federal Poverty Level

name: Weatherization Assistance Program (WAP)
description: Free home energy efficiency upgrades to reduce utility costs
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 200% of the Federal Poverty Level

name: Dollar Energy Fund
description: Utility bill payment assistance for qualifying households

name: Legal Services Corporation
description: Free civil legal aid for low-income Americans
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 125% of the Federal Poverty Level

name: LawHelp.org
description: Find free legal aid organizations and legal information by state
eligibilitySummary: Varies by local legal aid organization

name: American Bar Association Free Legal Help
description: Pro bono legal services directory from the ABA

name: NLADA (National Legal Aid & Defender Association)
description: National network of legal aid and public defender programs

name: Federal Pell Grants
description: College tuition grants for undergraduate students with financial need
eligibilitySummary: Undergraduate students with demonstrated financial need
incomeLimitNotes: Based on Expected Family Contribution (EFC); typically income under ~$60,000

name: GED Testing Service
description: GED preparation resources and testing for high school equivalency
eligibilitySummary: Anyone without a high school diploma

name: Khan Academy
description: Free online courses, lessons, and practice in many subjects

name: Coursera Financial Aid
description: Free access to online courses and certificates through financial aid

name: Public Libraries
description: Free access to internet, books, programs, and community resources
eligibilitySummary: Anyone — free with a library card

name: Head Start
description: Free early childhood education and family support for low-income families
eligibilitySummary: Families at or below the Federal Poverty Level, children ages 0-5
incomeLimitNotes: Income at or below 100% FPL; some programs serve up to 130% FPL

description: United Way's free social services hotline — call, text, or search online

name: National Domestic Violence Hotline
description: 24/7 confidential support for domestic violence survivors

name: RAINN (Rape, Abuse & Incest National Network)
description: Sexual assault hotline and online chat support

name: National Runaway Safeline
description: Crisis services and resources for youth who have run away or are at risk

name: Disaster Assistance (FEMA)
description: Federal disaster relief including housing, financial assistance, and crisis counseling
eligibilitySummary: Individuals affected by a federally declared disaster

name: The Trevor Project
description: Crisis intervention and suicide prevention for LGBTQ+ young people

name: VA Benefits
description: Central portal for all VA benefits including disability, education, and pension

name: VA Health Care
description: Medical services and health coverage for eligible veterans
eligibilitySummary: Veterans enrolled in VA health care (priority groups based on service and income)

name: HUD-VASH
description: Housing vouchers combined with VA case management for homeless veterans

name: Veterans Crisis Line
description: 24/7 crisis support for veterans, service members, and their families
eligibilitySummary: Veterans, service members, and their families

name: DAV (Disabled American Veterans)
description: Benefits assistance and advocacy for disabled veterans

name: CCDF (Child Care and Development Fund)
description: Subsidized childcare for working families through state-administered programs
eligibilitySummary: Working families with children, income limits vary by state
incomeLimitNotes: Income limits set by individual states; typically ≤ 85% State Median Income

name: National Diaper Bank Network
description: Find free diapers through a network of local diaper banks
eligibilitySummary: Families in need — no income verification typically required

name: Safe Haven Laws
description: Information about safely surrendering a newborn at designated locations
eligibilitySummary: Anyone — laws vary by state (typically infants up to 30 days old)

name: Child Welfare Information Gateway
description: Federal resource for child welfare, foster care, and family support information
eligibilitySummary: Anyone seeking child welfare information or support
